title: Nob Hill tenants demand action amid ongoing legal battle
summary: Nob Hill apartment tenants are escalating demands for action regarding persistent code violations and poor living conditions (lack of heat, maintenance issues, unhooked utilities). A city legal action initiated in April has been postponed five times; tenants are considering a rent strike and organizing collective pressure for the next hearing to proceed without further delays.
department_refs: Department of Neighborhood and Business Development

political_signal.description: Organized tenant activism demanding city follow through on enforcement action; potential rent strike indicates escalating pressure on both property management and municipal code enforcement responsiveness.

processing_notes: Article lacks specific building owner/management entity name; recommend CIO research Nob Hill property records
Five postponements of legal hearing suggest potential coordination/procedural bottleneck worth investigating
$600 credit to tenant for 2-month heat outage may indicate compliance audit gap
No CIO systems or digital services mentioned; potential opportunity for 311 complaint tracking integration
